creator: imp@FreeBSD.org
text:
But the best way to fix this would be to import NetBSD's cp's -N flag: don't copy flags. We don't need them. And I can think of no good that can come from copying them...

Though I'd really like to understand how they came to be set on the stable/14 system... If FreeBSD's installer or things are doing it, then we need to stop them from doing it. If it's a 3rd party backup program, we need to add a note to the release notes. If it can't be discovered why, then I'm unsure what to do.
